+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-206.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-206
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_12-25-03+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md — read-only reference for `PublishMode branch`, `NoPublish`, and the queue auto-publish gate.
+- ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md — read-only reference; used to distinguish the orchestrator dispatch lifecycle from the manual `TASK -> EXEC -> REVIEW -> CORRECT -> CLOSEOUT` chain.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 — inspected only (read-only); identified the `PublishMode branch` to `-NoPublish` propagation point at L897 inside the queue-drain block.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 — inspected only (read-only); identified the `[switch]$NoPublish` parameter at L53, the `$eligibleForPublish` computation at L1360, and the publish-skip branch at L1420-1422.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_TASK_2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300.md — read-only reference packet.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_CORRECT_2026-05-26_08-19-29+0300.md — read-only reference packet.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_2026-05-26_08-26-06+0300.md — read-only reference packet (round-1 EXEC, the dispatch's terminal executor report).
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-202/codex.control.round1.json — read-only reference for the Codex `pass` verdict and `ready_for_publish` commit-readiness on round 1.
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_08-31-34+0300.md — read-only reference for the ISSUE-202 queue-run loop output (`Dispatch loop finished`, `Codex control verdict: pass`, `Commit readiness: ready_for_publish`).
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-080531-502-373920.jsonl — read-only reference; shows `auto.tick: start (... mode=branch)`.
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-080613-969-372760.jsonl — read-only reference; shows `queue.publish: skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` followed by `queue.commit: committed (sha=00a5770)`.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_TASK_2026-05-26_08-54-58+0300.md — read-only reference packet.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_EXEC_2026-05-26_09-03-42+0300.md — read-only reference packet.
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-204/codex.control.round0.json — read-only reference for the Codex `pass` verdict and `ready_for_publish` commit-readiness on round 0.
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_09-09-19+0300.md — read-only reference for the ISSUE-204 queue-run loop output.
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-085405-952-392644.jsonl — read-only reference; shows `auto.tick: start (... mode=branch)`.
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-085452-616-397512.jsonl — read-only reference; shows `queue.publish: skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` followed by `queue.commit: committed (sha=f03989f)`.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-206_TASK_2026-05-26_12-19-20+0300.md — this dispatch's TASK packet (reference only, unedited).
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-206_EXEC_2026-05-26_12-25-03+0300.md — this execution report.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-206_TASK_2026-05-26_12-19-20+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch. Per `AGENTS.md` and the TASK
+packet, this is an orchestrator dispatch: no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet
+is emitted; the Codex control review is the gitignored run-dir JSON. The
+TASK packet is read-only (`MAY edit: None`) and instructs the executor to
+write only an `ISSUE-206` EXEC packet — that constraint is honored.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This dispatch is a read-only audit. No tracked source, test,
+  documentation, schema, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, queue, loop, or
+  task-brief file was modified.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added or modified.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added or modified.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+- No tracked file was modified by this dispatch. The only new files on disk
+  are this dispatch's own handoff artifacts: the TASK packet + sidecar (created
+  by the planner), this EXEC packet, and — once
+  `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` runs — its `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Protocol Note — Orchestrator Dispatches Do Not Produce REVIEW or CLOSEOUT Packets
+
+`AGENTS.md` (loaded by `CLAUDE.md`) is explicit: an orchestrator dispatch
+driven by `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` writes only a `TASK`, an `EXEC`, and
+optionally a `CORRECT` packet to `ai_handoffs/`. The Codex control review
+lives in the gitignored run-dir JSON
+(`.ai/dispatch-<DispatchId>/codex.control.round<N>.json`), never as a
+`_REVIEW_` packet. No `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is emitted, ever. Completion is
+signalled by the inner-loop `Dispatch loop finished.` message together with
+a Codex `pass` verdict and the queue-run commit on the dispatch's branch —
+*not* by a `_CLOSEOUT_` artifact.
+
+Consequence for ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204: the *absence* of `_REVIEW_` or
+`_CLOSEOUT_` packets in `ai_handoffs/` for those dispatches is not evidence
+of unfinished work. Both dispatches are by-design complete after their
+final EXEC packet + Codex `pass` verdict + queue-run commit landed on
+their respective `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branches.
+
+## Section 1 — `PublishMode branch` → `-NoPublish` Propagation in `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+
+The Auto driver translates `PublishMode` into queue arguments in the
+"Drain: run the hardened queue on the pending issue" block beginning around
+line 889. The propagation is three lines (L895-899) inside that block:
+
+```powershell
+# Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 L895-899
+$queueArgs = @('-NoProfile', '-ExecutionPolicy', 'Bypass', '-File', $queueScript,
+    '-MaxPlanRevisions', $MaxPlanRevisions, '-MaxCorrectionRounds', $MaxCorrectionRounds)
+if ($PublishMode -eq 'branch') { $queueArgs += '-NoPublish' }
+if ($TraceTiming) { $queueArgs += '-TraceTiming' }
+if ($EnablePreflightAudit) { $queueArgs += '-EnablePreflightAudit' }
+```
+
+Key points:
+
+- `$queueArgs` is the array splatted into `& powershell.exe @queueArgs` at
+  L905 to launch the queue runner as a child process.
+- L897 — `if ($PublishMode -eq 'branch') { $queueArgs += '-NoPublish' }` —
+  is the single site that translates the documented branch mode into the
+  Queue's `-NoPublish` switch. In `main` mode this append never executes,
+  so `-NoPublish` is not passed and Queue defaults to its auto-publish path.
+- L898 — `if ($TraceTiming) { $queueArgs += '-TraceTiming' }` — is an
+  **independent** conditional append controlled by the
+  `[switch]$TraceTiming` parameter declared at L79 in the Auto `param()`
+  block, with an environment-variable fallback at L86
+  (`$env:RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING -match '^(1|true|yes|on)$'`). Note
+  that the L898 conditional is gated on the parameter only, not the
+  env-var fallback, so the env-var fallback only enables Auto's own
+  in-process tracing and does not implicitly forward `-TraceTiming` to
+  Queue. Either way, `-TraceTiming` controls only the timing-trace JSONL
+  emission and has no relationship to publish policy.
+- L899 — `if ($EnablePreflightAudit) { $queueArgs += '-EnablePreflightAudit' }`
+  — is the third independent conditional, wired in by ISSUE-204. Like
+  `-TraceTiming` it is orthogonal to publish policy.
+
+Header context is consistent with the implementation: the script's
+comment-based help (read inline because the running session blocks
+`Get-Help` from cold-running the script under sandbox) declares
+`-PublishMode` at L34-35 with allowed values `branch` / `main` and the
+default `'branch'`, and `[ValidateSet('branch', 'main')]` /
+`[string]$PublishMode = 'branch'` at L63-64.
+
+Conclusion for Auto: the script does exactly what its help text says — in
+`branch` (default) mode it appends `-NoPublish` to the queue invocation,
+and in `main` mode it does not.
+
+## Section 2 — `NoPublish` Receipt and Consumption in `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+
+The Queue script accepts `-NoPublish` as a parameter and consumes it in the
+publish block at the end of a queue tick.
+
+Parameter declaration (L43-64, `param()` block):
+
+```powershell
+# Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 L53
+[switch]$NoPublish,
+```
+
+The script's comment-based help mentions `-NoPublish` at L41 ("Pushes only
+successful, Codex-control-passed dispatch commits. Use -NoPublish to keep
+the old local-branch-only behavior for a one-off run.").
+
+Publish-eligibility computation and consumption (L1360-1430, "Publish
+passed work" block):
+
+```powershell
+# Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 L1360
+$eligibleForPublish = ($committed -and $loopExit -eq 0 -and $verdict -eq 'pass')
+
+if ($eligibleForPublish -and -not $NoPublish) {
+    Write-Output "Codex control passed; publishing $branch to origin/main."
+    Write-TimingTrace "queue.publish: block-entry; eligibleForPublish=true"
+    # ... git fetch, ff-merge, push origin main, branch-delete ...
+} elseif ($eligibleForPublish -and $NoPublish) {
+    $publishDetail = "NoPublish set; kept $branch local."
+    Write-TimingTrace "queue.publish: skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)"
+} elseif ($committed) {
+    $publishFailed = $true
+    $publishDetail = "Not published because loop exit code was $loopExit and Codex control verdict was '$verdict'."
+    Write-TimingTrace "queue.publish: skipped (eligibleForPublish=false, loopExit=$loopExit, verdict=$verdict)"
+} else {
+    $publishDetail = "No branch commit was created."
+    Write-TimingTrace "queue.publish: skipped (committed=false)"
+}
+```
+
+Key points:
+
+- `$eligibleForPublish` (L1360) is computed *independently of* `$NoPublish`.
+  Its three inputs are `$committed` (the per-issue branch commit was
+  written), `$loopExit -eq 0` (the inner dispatch loop exited 0), and
+  `$verdict -eq 'pass'` (Codex control returned `pass`). This is the
+  success gate documented in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` §2 and §6 — "the
+  queue runner publishes only when the loop exits 0 and Codex control
+  verdict is `pass`."
+- The first `if` branch (L1362) — `if ($eligibleForPublish -and -not
+  $NoPublish)` — is the only path that runs the actual `git fetch` /
+  ff-merge / `git push origin main` / branch-delete sequence.
+- The second `elseif` branch (L1420-1422) — `elseif ($eligibleForPublish
+  -and $NoPublish)` — is the **publish-skip branch**: the run was eligible
+  by every other criterion, but `-NoPublish` was set, so the push is
+  deliberately skipped, the local branch is kept, and the trace line
+  `queue.publish: skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` is
+  emitted. Note that by this point the per-issue branch commit has already
+  been written (see L1438 — "Committed locally as `<sha>` on branch
+  `<branch>` (NoPublish set; not pushed)."). Skipping publish therefore
+  preserves the work on the dispatch branch for human review and merge.
+
+Independence from `-TraceTiming`: the Queue's `-TraceTiming` switch (L61)
+controls only the JSONL trace emission via `Write-TimingTrace` (L92-148).
+`Write-TimingTrace` no-ops when tracing is disabled and never affects
+control flow. The publish-eligibility computation and the publish-skip
+branch above do not read `-TraceTiming` at all; they read only
+`$committed`, `$loopExit`, `$verdict`, and `$NoPublish`. The trace line
+`queue.publish: skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` is
+emitted from inside the skip branch regardless of why the branch was
+taken — `-TraceTiming` only decides whether the line is materialized to
+the JSONL file. In short, `-TraceTiming` *exposes* the publish decision;
+it does not *make* the decision.
+
+## Section 3 — Reconciling ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204
+
+Both dispatches show exactly the documented contract above: they ran in
+branch mode, met the publish-eligibility gate, and Queue deliberately
+kept the work local because `-NoPublish` was set.
+
+### ISSUE-202 evidence
+
+- TASK packet
+  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_TASK_2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300.md`): planner
+  prompt grammar hardening; MAY edit limited to `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+  + the dispatch's own artifacts.
+- CORRECT packet
+  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_CORRECT_2026-05-26_08-19-29+0300.md`): single
+  approved correction (worked-example backtick-escaping defect) routed
+  back to the executor.
+- Round-1 EXEC packet
+  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_2026-05-26_08-26-06+0300.md`): single-
+  character escaping fix in the existing planner prompt literal;
+  canonical verify gate green; `git diff --check` exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-202/codex.control.round1.json`: `"verdict":"pass"`,
+  `"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish"`. (Round 0 was
+  `"needs_changes"`, which is why a `CORRECT` packet exists for this
+  dispatch.)
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_08-31-34+0300.md`: queue-run log shows
+  the loop output `Codex control round 1: pass`, `Dispatch loop
+  finished.`, `Codex control verdict: pass`, `Commit readiness:
+  ready_for_publish`, and `No commit or push was performed.` at the
+  loop level (publish is owned by the queue, not the loop).
+- Auto trace
+  (`.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-080531-502-373920.jsonl`):
+  first event `auto.tick: start (PID=373920, repo=RustCADs/RGE,
+  mode=branch)`. The Auto driver entered in branch mode, which per L897
+  guarantees `-NoPublish` was appended to `$queueArgs`.
+- Queue trace
+  (`.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-080613-969-372760.jsonl`):
+  shows `queue.control: verdict-read (verdict=pass, execStatus=executed)`
+  → `queue.commit: committed (sha=00a5770)` → `queue.publish: skipped
+  (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` → label/comment cleanup.
+
+The verdict triplet (`committed=true`, `loopExit=0`,
+`verdict=pass`) makes `$eligibleForPublish=true`. The Auto driver passed
+`-NoPublish` because branch mode is its default, so the Queue took the
+publish-skip branch — exactly the documented behavior.
+
+### ISSUE-204 evidence
+
+- TASK packet
+  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_TASK_2026-05-26_08-54-58+0300.md`): wire the
+  already-implemented `EnablePreflightAudit` switch through Auto and
+  Queue; MAY edit limited to `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` plus the dispatch's own artifacts.
+- Round-0 EXEC packet
+  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_EXEC_2026-05-26_09-03-42+0300.md`): added
+  `[switch]$EnablePreflightAudit` to both scripts and the conditional
+  appends; refactored the Queue's Loop invocation into a splatted args
+  array; canonical verify gate green; `git diff --check` exit 0; the
+  Loop script untouched.
+- `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-204/codex.control.round0.json`: `"verdict":"pass"`,
+  `"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish"`. (No CORRECT round was
+  needed — round 0 passed directly.)
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_09-09-19+0300.md`: loop output
+  `Codex control round 0: pass`, `Dispatch loop finished.`, `Codex
+  control verdict: pass`, `Commit readiness: ready_for_publish`, `No
+  commit or push was performed.`.
+- Auto trace
+  (`.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-085405-952-392644.jsonl`):
+  first event `auto.tick: start (PID=392644, repo=RustCADs/RGE,
+  mode=branch)`. Branch mode again, so `-NoPublish` was appended.
+- Queue trace
+  (`.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-085452-616-397512.jsonl`):
+  shows `queue.control: verdict-read (verdict=pass, execStatus=executed)`
+  → `queue.commit: committed (sha=f03989f)` → `queue.publish: skipped
+  (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` → label/comment cleanup.
+
+Same pattern as ISSUE-202: branch mode → `-NoPublish` appended → publish
+deliberately skipped after a passing control verdict.
+
+### Plain-language summary
+
+`eligibleForPublish=true` means the run met every gate that would publish
+in `main` mode: it produced a branch commit, the inner dispatch loop
+exited 0, and Codex control returned `pass`. `NoPublish=true` means the
+Auto driver was in `branch` mode (its documented default) and therefore
+instructed Queue to keep the commit local. The trace line `queue.publish:
+skipped (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)` is the design's "the
+work passed and is ready, but branch mode says don't push" signal — a
+*successful* skip, not a failure. The local branch commit (visible at
+`queue.commit: committed (sha=...)` in both Queue trace files) is what
+the human is expected to review and merge.
+
+## Classification
+
+CLASSIFICATION: intended documented behavior
+
+## Smallest Safe Follow-up
+
+- **Action:** A human closes GitHub issue #206 after reviewing this audit.
+- **Allowed files:** none. This is a documentation/triage step on the GitHub
+  side only; no repository file is edited.
+- **Verification gates:** `git diff --check` (expected exit 0), and
+  `git status --short --untracked-files=all` (expected to show no
+  tracked-file changes from the issue-close step).
+- **Halt condition:** any proposed source, docs, scripts, schema, Cargo,
+  workflow, or task-brief edit — including any change to script help text,
+  trace wording, variable names, queue logic, publish behavior, GitHub
+  labels, branches, commits, or other issue mutations — would exceed this
+  follow-up's scope; the next executor must halt and return
+  `NEEDS_HUMAN` rather than expand it.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- Static read of `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` — readable; documents
+  `PublishMode branch` as the default, "human-gated branch mode" (§7.1,
+  §17.4) and the publish auto-gate "the queue runner publishes only when
+  the loop exits 0 and Codex control verdict is `pass`" (§2, §6).
+- Static read of `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` — readable; describes
+  the manual `TASK -> EXEC -> REVIEW -> CORRECT -> CLOSEOUT` chain, which
+  is the chain that does *not* apply to orchestrator dispatches (per
+  `AGENTS.md`).
+- Inspection of `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` — comment-based help block at

+  `param()` block at L62-82 declares `[string]$PublishMode = 'branch'`

+- Inspection of `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` — comment-based help block at

+  the `param()` block at L43-64 declares `[switch]$NoPublish` (L53),
+  `[switch]$TraceTiming` (L61), and `[switch]$EnablePreflightAudit`
+  (L63); the publish block at L1360-1430 computes `$eligibleForPublish`

+  `$eligibleForPublish -and $NoPublish` (L1420-1422).
+- Inspection of the ISSUE-202 packets, run-dir scratch, dispatch log, and

+- Inspection of the ISSUE-204 packets, run-dir scratch, dispatch log, and

+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace or conflict-marker issues).
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` →

+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Perform a read-only automation audit for GitHub issue #206: explain the
+`PublishMode branch` to `NoPublish` propagation path, reconcile the
+ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204 trace records that show `NoPublish=true` together
+with `eligibleForPublish=true`, and classify the behavior as one of:
+intended documented behavior, docs/help/name mismatch, implementation bug,
+or NEEDS_HUMAN. This dispatch must not change the automation implementation,
+tests, docs, schemas, Cargo metadata, workflows, task brief, or any prior
+handoff artifacts.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is a read-only audit; do not modify tracked source, tests, docs,
+  Cargo files, workflows, schemas, task brief, or automation scripts.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`

+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `crates/**`

+- `runtime/**`

+- Any tracked file not explicitly generated for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-206_EXEC_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-206_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`
+- `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-206/**`
+- `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-*.jsonl`
+- `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-*.jsonl`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source, test, doc, schema, Cargo, workflow, task brief, or automation
+  files.
+- New handoff packets for any dispatch other than `ISSUE-206`.
+- Persistent extracted logs, ZIP files, scratch reports, screenshots, or
+  copied artifacts in the repository. If temporary files are required, place
+  them outside the repo and delete them before writing the EXEC report.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- An `ISSUE-206` EXEC report containing a concise evidence-backed audit.
+- A section naming where `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` translates
+  `PublishMode branch` into Queue arguments. It must identify the
+  `queueArgs` construction and the `-NoPublish` append for branch mode, and
+  distinguish the independent `-TraceTiming` append.
+- A section naming where `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` receives and consumes
+  `NoPublish`. It must identify the `[switch]$NoPublish` parameter, the
+  independent `$eligibleForPublish = ($committed -and $loopExit -eq 0 -and
+  $verdict -eq 'pass')` computation, and the publish branch that skips
+  pushing when both `$eligibleForPublish` and `$NoPublish` are true.
+- A section explaining ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204: Auto trace records show
+  `mode=branch`, Queue trace records show `queue.publish: skipped
+  (NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true)`, and the dispatch logs/control
+  JSON show loop exit 0, Codex `pass`, and `ready_for_publish`. Explain that
+  `eligibleForPublish=true` means the run met the success gate that would
+  publish in main mode; `NoPublish=true` means branch mode intentionally kept
+  the commit local.
+- A classification line with exactly one value from this set:
+  `intended documented behavior`, `docs/help/name mismatch`,
+  `implementation bug`, `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- Exactly one smallest safe follow-up. If the classification is intended
+  documented behavior and no code/doc fix is justified, the follow-up must be:
+  human closes issue #206 after reviewing this audit. Allowed files: none.
+  Verification gates: `git diff --check` and
+  `git status --short --untracked-files=all`. Halt condition: any proposed
+  source, docs, scripts, schema, Cargo, workflow, or task-brief edit. If the
+  evidence does not support that no-change follow-up, return `NEEDS_HUMAN`
+  instead of inventing a broader fix.
+- A final verification section reporting `git diff --check` and `git status`
+  output, with an explicit statement that no tracked source/test/doc/Cargo/
+  workflow/schema/task-brief/script changes were made.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The audit explicitly states that orchestrator dispatches do not require
+  `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets; do not treat their absence in ISSUE-202
+  or ISSUE-204 as evidence of unfinished work.
+- The audit cites all required local evidence:
+  `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`; the comment-based help text in
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`; the
+  implementation bodies of both scripts; ISSUE-202 handoff packets,
+  `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-202/` scratch, dispatch log, and trace JSONL; and
+  ISSUE-204 handoff packets, `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-204/` scratch, dispatch log,
+  and trace JSONL.
+- The audit identifies the Auto propagation point as
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` around the queue drain block where branch mode
+  appends `-NoPublish` to `$queueArgs`.
+- The audit identifies the Queue consumption point as
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` around the publish block where
+  `$eligibleForPublish` is computed before the `$NoPublish` skip branch.
+- The audit explains why `TraceTiming` only exposes the trace lines and does
+  not change publish policy.
+- The audit classifies the observed ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204 behavior. The
+  expected classification is `intended documented behavior` unless the
+  executor finds contradictory evidence in the required artifacts.
+- The EXEC report names exactly one smallest safe follow-up with allowed files,
+  verification gates, and halt conditions, or returns `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- No source, test, documentation, Cargo, workflow, schema, task brief, prior
+  handoff packet, or automation script is modified.
+- Only this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts plus optional ignored `.ai`
+  dispatch scratch remain as new files.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement a fix.
+- Do not update script help, docs, trace wording, variable names, queue logic,
+  publish behavior, GitHub labels, branches, commits, or issue comments.
+- Do not run publish, push, merge, branch-delete, label-edit, or GitHub issue
+  mutation commands.
+- Do not infer dispatch state from `ai_handoffs/` listings alone. Read the
+  automation spec and packet protocol first, then inspect the run artifacts.
+- Do not run the full repository verification gate manually for this audit.
+  The task-specific gates are read-only diff/status checks; the orchestrator
+  may still run its canonical gate automatically after execution.
+- Treat any unrelated dirty worktree state as out of scope. Do not revert or
+  clean changes you did not make; report them if they affect the audit.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `Get-Content -Raw AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` -> expected readable; use it to

+- `Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` -> expected readable;

+- Inspect the script help text for both automation scripts. Acceptable commands

+- Inspect `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` with a
+  read-only command such as `Select-String` for `PublishMode`, `NoPublish`,
+  `eligibleForPublish`, `TraceTiming`, and `queue.publish`.
+- Inspect the ISSUE-202 artifacts at minimum:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_TASK_2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_CORRECT_2026-05-26_08-19-29+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_2026-05-26_08-26-06+0300.md`,

+  `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-202/verification.round1.log`,
+  `ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_08-31-34+0300.md`,

+  and
+  `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-080613-969-372760.jsonl`.
+- Inspect the ISSUE-204 artifacts at minimum:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_TASK_2026-05-26_08-54-58+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-204_EXEC_2026-05-26_09-03-42+0300.md`,
+  `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-204/codex.control.round0.json`,
+  `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-204/verification.round0.log`,
+  `ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_09-09-19+0300.md`,
+  `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-085405-952-392644.jsonl`,
+  and
+  `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-085452-616-397512.jsonl`.
+- `git diff --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` -> expected no tracked
+  source/test/doc/Cargo/workflow/schema/task-brief/script changes; new files,
+  if any, are limited to this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts and ignored
+  `.ai` scratch.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt and return `NEEDS_HUMAN` without editing any source,
+docs, scripts, schemas, task brief, workflows, Cargo files, tests, or prior
+handoff artifacts if any of the following occur:
+
+- Required ISSUE-202 or ISSUE-204 artifacts are missing or contradict the
+  trace/story enough that the classification cannot be made confidently.
+- The evidence suggests `PublishMode branch` failed to pass `-NoPublish`, or
+  Queue would push to `origin/main` despite `NoPublish=true`.
+- Explaining the behavior would require changing implementation, docs, script
+  help, trace naming, schemas, task brief, tests, workflows, Cargo metadata, or
+  prior artifacts.
+- Any GitHub mutation, push, merge, branch deletion, label change, or issue
+  comment seems necessary.
+- `git diff --check` fails for a reason caused by this dispatch.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` shows new or modified files
+  outside this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts and optional ignored `.ai`
+  scratch, unless they are clearly pre-existing unrelated user changes.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Initial planner audit found this expected path: `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+documents `PublishMode branch` as human-gated branch mode and appends
+`-NoPublish` to Queue only when `$PublishMode -eq 'branch'`; it appends
+`-TraceTiming` independently. `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` exposes
+`[switch]$NoPublish`, computes `$eligibleForPublish` from successful commit,
+loop exit 0, and Codex `pass`, and then skips the publish block when
+`$NoPublish` is set. ISSUE-202 and ISSUE-204 trace files both show Auto
+starting in `mode=branch` and Queue skipping publish with
+`NoPublish=true, eligibleForPublish=true`; their dispatch logs/control JSON
+show successful control-passed work ready for publish. This points to
+intended documented behavior, not a publish bug, but the Executor must
+re-check the evidence and report the final classification.
